_meta.notes: First town west of Caelum, on the road to the Long Indices. A river-mile of cataracts (the source of the river Indice) drives a row of paper-mills — the Cataract Mile mills produce nearly all of the paper-fiber the orcish papermakers use. The town's noise is constant water; the mill-workers communicate in hand-signs.

tagline: A mile of waterfalls turning a row of paper-mills. Don't try to talk over it.
ambientPool: The roar of falling water never quite recedes.
A mill-worker signs to another across the courtyard — a question, an answer, a laugh in three motions.
Pulped reed slurry drips off a freshly pressed sheet.
An orcish wagon waits at the gate; the load will go to the Long Indices by dawn.

rooms.summary: Stone courtyard at the head of the mile. The first mill's wheel turns visibly through an arch in the back wall. Carts wait for loads; signers move between them.
ambientPool: A signer relays a count from the loading dock to the mill-master in five quick gestures.
An orcish customer reads a contract in the relative quiet of a covered alcove.
A child practices the sign for 'two hundred' over and over.
The water never lets up. You stop noticing it after an hour. Then you notice it again at sundown.

exits.narrative: Through the back arch onto the pulp floor where the slurry is made.
Up the wooden stair to the drying loft where the sheets cure.

rooms.summary: Long stone-floored hall under the mill wheels. Reed-fiber from Tallowfen and tallow-fen reed-pulp from upriver are mixed here in great vats. Wet, warm, loud.
ambientPool: A vat is stirred with a paddle as long as a person.
Two pulpers compare a handful of slurry without speaking — one nods, the other adds water.
A mill-master taps a bench with one knuckle; everyone within sight knows what it means.
The wheel's rhythm rules the floor.

rooms.summary: Wooden loft above the mill where pressed sheets dry on slatted racks. Quieter than the floor — the wheel-noise dampens through the floorboards.
ambientPool: An apprentice turns a sheet on its rack with a careful gloved hand.
An orcish Shelver inspects a finished bundle and nods without speaking.
The light through the slats falls in stripes across the racks.
A signer pokes their head up from below to ask a question; the loft replies in sign and goes back to work.
